name: Cheese Ravioli

ingredients.item: can of San Marzano tomatoes plus one can water
00 flour plus extra to handle dough

method: Crack eggs into the hole and mix the egg part with your hands. Scoop the flour towards the egg mixture and slowly mix, it’s a little tricky but practice controlling the eggs so they don’t spread across your work space. Knead the flour into the eggs until well mixed and it starts to form a ball, kneading and turning the dough as you would when you make bread. The perfect dough will be easily pliable and a little bit soft. If your dough seems too stiff you can add a little water to make it easier to knead. Form a ball, cover with a small bowl and let rest.

In the meantime, set up your pasta machine and flour a large cutting board. After 5 minutes remove dough from bowl and break ball in half, flattening the half with your hands. Flour both sides to avoid sticking, and run the dough through pasta machine to make it flat, start at 6-7 and go down to 3. Sprinkle dough strips with flour on both sides and lay on a cutting board. Make two flat long pieces roughly the same size. Scoop 1 T of cheese filling two inches apart, and continue down the strip. Take your top piece of pasta and lay over the strip and cheese. With fingers dipped in water, form the center of the dough around the cheese. With a ravioli cutter center the middle cheese part and press down. Always sprinkle flour on your work surface otherwise it could stick and become messy. Lift with a spatula and transfer to a floured surface. Line up your pasta and let it dry. You can store the dried ravioli in an airtight container in your freezer, if you would like to make it for a later time.

In a frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil and crushed garlic clove, saute for a minute. Add, tomatoes, water, salt, pepper and 4 torn basil leaves. Let simmer for 20 minutes. Now, boil a pot of salted water, add ravioli and cook until they rise to the surface. Remove carefully and transfer to simmering sauce.
Mix and manticare a couple times. Arrange ravioli and sprinkle grated parmesan on top, and garnish with a basil leaf. Serves 2. Serving suggestion: bruschetta and salad! Olé
